$statusLegend.ported: byte-equivalent to upstream (CI fails on drift)
$statusLegend.adapted: TypeScript port and/or namespace swap; logically equivalent
$statusLegend.extended: fork adds behavior on top of upstream
$statusLegend.added: file unique to the fork (no upstream)

files.notes: name + textdomain swapped to artisanpack namespace; supports/attributes verbatim.
TypeScript port. Adds explicit 'wp-block-paragraph' class to useBlockProps so the editor canvas matches frontend without depending on __experimentalSelector internals.
TypeScript port; class list adds 'wp-block-paragraph' explicitly (parity with edit).
Upstream raw-transform preserved; added bidirectional block transforms for core/paragraph ↔ artisanpack/paragraph round-tripping.
TypeScript port; renamed file to match the exported hook.
Sass variables resolved inline ($block-bg-padding--v → 1.25em, --h → 2.375em). data-type selectors rewritten from 'core/paragraph' to 'artisanpack/paragraph'.
Inline SVG for the inserter (editor doesn't bundle dashicons).
Adapted to the visual-editor auto-discovery contract (custom-blocks.ts glob). Upstream index.js wires `init` and PHP server-side init; we don't need either.

knownDivergences: edit.tsx + save.tsx add 'wp-block-paragraph' to useBlockProps explicitly. Upstream relies on the __experimentalSelector private path to inject this. We prefer the explicit class so renderers (Blade/React/Vue) don't have to special-case the namespace.
transforms.ts adds bidirectional block transforms that don't exist upstream — the fork must coexist with core/paragraph in mixed documents during the V2 rollout.
index.ts replaces upstream's PHP-side init wiring with the visual-editor auto-discovery contract.

use-enter.ts adds a defensive null-check + parent-equality guard inside the ancestor-climbing while-loop. Upstream omits both; the fork's guard prevents a theoretical infinite loop when no ancestor accepts the block.
